whoami.name: whoami - Display operator identification and profile.
whoami.description: Displays comprehensive information about the portfolio owner, including name, title, a detailed biography, main focus areas, and links to key online presences (GitHub, LinkedIn, Email).
examples: whoami              - Shows the full user profile.
whoami.notes: Output is formatted into sections: IDENTIFICATION, PROFILE BRIEF, PRIMARY DIRECTIVES, NETWORK ACCESS.

skills.name: skills - List a summary of key skills.
skills.description: Displays a summarized list of the portfolio owner's key skills, categorized for overview. For detailed exploration, use the 'skilltree' command.
examples: skills             - Shows the summary of key skills.

skilltree.name: skilltree - Interactively explore a hierarchy of technical skills.
skilltree.description: Displays a tree-like structure of skills. Use without arguments to show top-level categories, or with a path to navigate to a specific skill area. Paths can use skill names or their defined aliases (e.g., 'se' for Software Engineering).
skilltree.arguments: [path]: Optional. A path to a specific skill category. Aliases are preferred for brevity.
examples: skilltree          - Shows all top-level skill categories.
skilltree se       - Shows skills under Software Engineering.
skilltree ai       - Shows skills under AI & Machine Learning.
skilltree.notes: Refer to 'public/config/content/skills.json' for defined names and aliases.

hobbies.name: hobbies - List hobbies and interests.
hobbies.description: Displays the portfolio owner's hobbies and interests, categorized for easy reading.
examples: hobbies            - Shows the list of hobbies and interests.

contact.name: contact - Display contact information.
contact.description: Shows all channels to get in touch. Each contact method is presented as a labeled, clickable link.
examples: contact            - Displays all contact information.
contact.notes: Includes Email, LinkedIn, GitHub, and Medium (if configured).

mission.name: mission - Display recruiter dossier.
mission.description: Displays a professional dossier summary with identity, role, and status. Includes clickable quick links for CV, LinkedIn, GitHub, and Email. Alias: 'hire'.
examples: mission            - Shows the recruiter dossier with quick links.
hire               - Same as mission.
mission.notes: Recruiter mode can also be triggered via #recruiter in the URL or ?mode=recruiter query parameter.

download.name: download - Download files.
download.description: Initiates the download of a specified file. Currently supports downloading the CV.
examples: download cv        - Attempts to download the CV.
download.notes: The CV link is configured in js/config/index.js. Browser pop-up blockers might interfere; the nav bar also contains a CV link.

theme.name: theme - Change the color theme.
theme.description: Switches the visual theme of the terminal and rain animation. Running without arguments lists available themes and shows the current one. Theme choice is saved and restored on reload.
theme.arguments: [theme_name]: Optional. Name of the theme to apply.
examples: theme              - Shows current theme and available options.
theme amber        - Switches to the amber theme.
theme neuralstorm  - Switches to the neural-storm theme.
theme.available_themes: amber, crimson, ember, ghost, green (default), inferno, midnight, neuralstorm, phantom, reloaded, sakura.
theme.notes: Theme changes also update the rain colors. Use 'reset' to revert to defaults.

rain.name: rain - Configure the Matrix rain animation.
rain.description:
Umbrella command for all rain customisation. Run 'rain' with no arguments to see current config. Each subcommand controls a different aspect of the rain.

Subcommands:
  preset   — Apply a named visual preset
  font     — Switch the glyph set
  size     — Set glyph pixel size
  gravity  — Control fall acceleration
  glyphspeed — Control glyph mutation rate
rain.arguments:
preset <name>             — Apply a preset. Options: default, comet, storm, whisper, ancient, emberfall, rainfall, frozen.
font <name>               — Switch font set: classic, resurrections, combined.
size <px> | reset         — Set glyph size (8–40px), or reset to default.
gravity <level> | off     — Acceleration levels: moon, earth, jupiter.
glyphspeed <1-30> | reset — Mutation rate. Lower = faster churn.
examples: rain                   - Shows current rain config.
rain preset storm      - Applies the 'storm' preset.
rain preset default    - Resets rain to default.
rain font classic      - Switches to the classic 1999 glyph set.
rain size 20           - Sets glyph size to 20px.
rain size reset        - Resets glyph size to default (16px).
rain gravity earth     - Natural gravitational pull.
rain gravity off       - Disables gravity.
rain glyphspeed 15     - Glyphs hold steady (deliberate pace).
rain glyphspeed 1      - Rapid character churn.
rain.notes: Preset choice is saved to localStorage and restored on reload. Gravity and glyphspeed are session-only — they reset when the preset changes.

term.name: term - Configure the terminal appearance.
term.description:
Umbrella command for all terminal appearance settings. Run 'term' with no arguments to see current config.

Subcommands:
  opacity  — Adjust background transparency
  fontsize — Change text size
  size     — Resize the terminal window
term.arguments:
opacity <0-100 | reset>           — Set background opacity (e.g., 70 or 0.7).
fontsize <size | small | default | large> — Set font size (e.g., 13px, large).
size <W> <H> | reset              — Resize terminal (e.g., 60vw 70vh), or reset.
examples: term                       - Shows current terminal config.
term opacity 80            - Sets terminal background to 80% opacity.
term opacity reset         - Resets opacity to default.
term fontsize large        - Sets font to large preset.
term fontsize 13px         - Sets font to 13 pixels.
term size 70vw 60vh        - Resizes terminal.
term size reset            - Resets terminal to default size.
term.notes: Valid CSS units for size: px, %, vw, vh, em, rem.

toggleterm.name: toggleterm - Hide or show the terminal.
toggleterm.description: Toggles the visibility of the terminal window with a fade animation.
examples: toggleterm         - Hides terminal if visible, shows if hidden.

help.name: help - Display available commands.
help.description: Lists all commands with short descriptions. For detailed documentation on any command, use 'man <command>'.
examples: help               - Shows the command list.
help.notes: Easter egg commands are intentionally hidden from this list. Type 'ee' to see them.

man.name: man - Display manual pages for commands.
man.description: Shows the detailed manual page for a command: name, synopsis, description, arguments, examples, and notes. Run 'man' without arguments to list all available pages.
man.arguments: <command_name>: The command to look up. For umbrella commands, 'man rain' covers all rain subcommands.
examples: man rain           - Full documentation for all rain subcommands.
man term           - Full documentation for all term subcommands.
man theme          - Manual for the theme command.
man skilltree      - Manual for the skilltree command.

clear.name: clear - Clear terminal output.
clear.description: Clears all output from the terminal screen. The welcome message is redisplayed.
examples: clear              - Clears the terminal screen.

search.name: search - Fuzzy search across content.
search.description: Searches across skills, hobbies, and command names using Levenshtein distance for fuzzy matching. Useful for finding relevant commands or content without knowing exact names.
search.arguments: [keyword]: Optional. The term to search for.
examples: search python      - Finds skills and commands related to 'python'.
search music       - Finds hobbies related to 'music'.
search rain        - Finds all rain-related commands.

date.name: date - Display current date and time.
date.description: Shows the current local date and time. If a timezone alias is provided, it displays the time for that timezone.
date.arguments: [timezone_alias]: Optional. A short alias: utc, gmt, est, pst, ist, jst, aest, bst, cet.
examples: date               - Shows current local date and time.
date utc           - Shows Coordinated Universal Time.
date ist           - Shows Indian Standard Time.

screenshot.name: screenshot - Capture the Matrix rain canvas.
screenshot.description: Captures a high-resolution PNG of the current rain animation. Does not capture the terminal or UI — rain canvas only.
examples: screenshot         - Captures at 1920×1080.
screenshot 2k      - Captures at 2560×1440.
screenshot.notes: File is named automatically with preset, theme, and timestamp.

reset.name: reset - Reset all preferences to defaults.
reset.description: Clears all saved preferences from localStorage and restores: green theme, default rain preset, and default rain/terminal settings. Session-only settings (gravity, glyphspeed) are also cleared.
examples: reset              - Restores everything to factory defaults.

decode.name: decode - Decode-reveal text with a Matrix effect.
decode.description: Renders text as scrambled glyphs that resolve into readable characters — a Matrix-style decryption animation. Without arguments, decodes a random Matrix quote.
decode.arguments: [text]: Optional. Custom text to decode. Without args, uses a random quote.
examples: decode                       - Decodes a random Matrix quote.
decode hello world           - Decodes the text 'hello world'.

sudo.name: sudo - Attempt a superuser command.
sudo.description: A thematic command. Attempts to run a command with superuser privileges, which is always denied in this simulated terminal.
sudo.arguments: <command>: Any command string you wish to 'attempt' with sudo.
examples: sudo reboot        - Attempts to reboot (denied).
sudo.notes: For thematic effect only. Listed under the 'ee' (easter eggs) command.

ask.name: ask - Ask a plain-English question.
ask.description: Maps a natural-language question to the most relevant command using local keyword matching (no AI/LLM — just string matching). Multi-word input that isn't a recognised command is routed here automatically.
ask.arguments: <question>: A plain-English question, e.g. 'what have you built'.
examples: ask what have you built   - Routes to the projects/dossier.
ask how do I reach you    - Routes to contact.
ask.notes: Routing rules are defined in js/config/index.js (ask.routes).

changelog.name: changelog - Show the project's release history.
changelog.description: Renders the site's own CHANGELOG.md in the terminal — the version history of this portfolio.
examples: changelog          - Shows all releases.
changelog.notes: Sourced from CHANGELOG.md at build time, so it never drifts from the repo.
